message:
fix: correct vmap batch-trace constant broadcasting and align threefry PRNG with JAX

fj-dispatch: Pass lead_len into execute_vmap_batch_trace so unbatched
outputs (constants that don't depend on the mapped input) are properly
broadcast via stack_axis0 to match the batch dimension, instead of
being returned as bare scalars. Adds a test that vmaps a constant
body (1+2) over a 3-element vector and asserts shape [3] output.

fj-lax/threefry: Three correctness fixes to match JAX's reference
implementation:
- random_fold_in: swap argument order to [0, data] matching JAX's
  threefry_seed which places high-32 bits first (always 0 for u32).
- generate_bits: rewrite to JAX's _threefry_random_bits_original
  algorithm — linear iota split in half as (x0, x1) inputs, output
  concatenated as [y0, y1], truncated to count.
- random_uniform: generate 2×count u32s, combine pairs into u64,
  mask to 52 mantissa bits with 1.0 exponent for proper [0,1)
  double-precision conversion (was using u32/2^32 single-precision
  approximation).
- random_normal: replace Box-Muller with JAX's erfinv approach —
  uniform in (-1,1) then sqrt(2)*erfinv(u).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
